title: Global Dietary Database (GDD) 2018 Final Estimates — Country-Level Dietary & Nutrient Intake (incl. Seafood)
abstract: Modeled, survey-derived estimates of habitual dietary intake for 46+ foods, beverages, and nutrients, disaggregated by country, age, sex, urbanicity, and education level. The local archive contains the full country-level, global, and regional estimate releases (one CSV per dietary factor, e.g. v11 = Total seafoods, v30 = Seafood omega-3 (n-3) fat), plus the official codebook. For the Red Sea nutrition-health domain, the seafood intake (v11) and seafood omega-3 fat (v30) variables for Saudi Arabia (ISO3 SAU) are the primary variables of interest, but the archive includes all other GDD dietary factors as well.

variables.name: median
variables.label: Total Seafood Intake (median)
variables.description: Modeled median habitual daily intake of total seafoods for the given country/age/sex/urbanicity/education/year stratum, with 95% uncertainty interval in upperci_95/lowerci_95.

variables.name: serving
variables.label: Total Seafood Intake (servings)
variables.description: Modeled median habitual daily intake of total seafoods expressed in standardized servings, with s_lowerci_95/s_upperci_95 uncertainty bounds.

variables.name: iso3
variables.label: Country ISO3 Code
variables.description: ISO3 country code; Saudi Arabia is 'SAU'.

resources.name: Seafood omega-3 (n-3) fat intake, country-level estimates (v30_cnty.csv)

variables.name: median
variables.label: Seafood Omega-3 Fat Intake (median)
variables.description: Modeled median habitual daily intake of omega-3 (n-3) fatty acids derived specifically from seafood, with 95% uncertainty interval.

resources.name: GDD 2018 Codebook
resources.path: KAUST - DATA/Human Nutrition and Health/GDD_FinalEstimates_01102022/GDD 2018 Codebook_Jan 10 2022.xlsx

steward.name: KAUST Human Nutrition and Health Data Team

access.how_to_request: Publicly available from the Global Dietary Database (globaldietarydatabase.org) after free registration and acceptance of the GDD data use terms; no restriction on redistribution of derived summary statistics.

provenance.originator: Global Dietary Database (GDD), Tufts University Friedman School of Nutrition Science and Policy
provenance.license: GDD Data Use Agreement (free registration required)
provenance.citation: Global Dietary Database (GDD). GDD 2018 Final Estimates, released January 2022. Boston, MA: Tufts University Friedman School of Nutrition Science and Policy.

known_issues: Estimates are model-based (survey-calibrated Bayesian hierarchical models), not direct measurements; country-level values for Saudi Arabia may rely partly on regional/superregion borrowing of strength where local survey data are sparse.
No sub-national or Red-Sea-specific spatial resolution is available; all values are national aggregates for Saudi Arabia, applied uniformly across all five subbasins.

The local archive contains ~46 additional dietary-factor CSVs per estimate level (Country-level/Global/Regional) beyond the two seafood-related variables itemized here (e.g. fruits, vegetables, meats, dairy, energy, macro/micronutrients); only v11 (Total seafoods) and v30 (Seafood omega-3 fat) are cataloged as resources given the domain focus, but the full folder is present locally.

This source was tagged '[New]' (aspirational, not yet acquired) in the team's Sources - NH.Rmd registry, but the full dataset (~5.7 GB) is already present locally — the Rmd tag appears stale relative to actual holdings.
